audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for unjust steward, Mammon, law/prophets, divorce saying, rich man and Lazarus, Abraham's bosom, Hades/depths, reversal and Moses/prophets warning.

verses.restored: He also said to the disciples, A certain rich person had a steward, and this one was accused to him as squandering his possessions.
Calling him, he said, What is this I hear about you? Give the account of your stewardship, for you are no longer able to steward.
The steward said within himself, What shall I do, because my lord is taking the stewardship away from me? I am not strong enough to dig; I am ashamed to beg.
I know what I will do, so that when I am removed from stewardship, people may receive me into their houses.
Calling each one of his lord's debtors, he said to the first, How much do you owe my lord?
He said, A hundred baths of oil. He said to him, Take your bill, sit quickly, and write fifty.
Then to another he said, And you, how much do you owe? He said, A hundred cors of wheat. He says to him, Take your bill and write eighty.
The lord praised the steward of injustice because he acted shrewdly; for the children of this age are more shrewd toward their own generation than the children of light.
And I say to you, make friends for yourselves by means of Mammon of injustice, so that when it fails, they may receive you into age-long tents.
The one trustworthy in least is trustworthy also in much, and the one unjust in least is unjust also in much.
If then you have not become trustworthy with unjust Mammon, who will entrust to you the true?
If you have not become trustworthy with what belongs to another, who will give you what is your own?

No household servant is able to serve two lords; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will hold fast to one and despise the other. You are not able to serve the Divine and Mammon.
The Pharisees, being lovers of silver, heard all these things and were ridiculing him.
He said to them, You are the ones justifying yourselves before humans, but the Divine knows your hearts; because what is high among humans is an abomination before the Divine.
The Law and the Prophets were until John; from then the good news of the reign of the Divine is announced, and everyone is pressing into it.
It is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one stroke of the Law to fall.
Everyone releasing his wife and marrying another commits adultery, and the one marrying a woman released from a husband commits adultery.
There was a certain rich person, clothed in purple and fine linen, making merry splendidly every day.
A certain poor person named Lazarus had been laid at his gate, covered with sores,
longing to be filled from the things falling from the rich person's table; even the dogs came and licked his sores.
It happened that the poor person died and was carried away by the messengers to Abraham's bosom. The rich person also died and was buried.
In Hades/depths, being in torment, he lifted up his eyes and saw Abraham from far away and Lazarus in his bosom.
He called out, Father Abraham, have mercy on me and send Lazarus so that he may dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because I am in anguish in this flame.
Abraham said, Child, remember that you received your good things in your life, and Lazarus likewise harmful things; now here he is comforted, and you are in anguish.
Besides all these things, between us and you a great chasm has been fixed, so that those wanting to pass from here to you are not able, nor may they cross from there to us.
He said, Then I ask you, father, that you send him to my father's house,
for I have five brothers, so that he may warn them, lest they also come into this place of anguish.
Abraham says, They have Moses and the Prophets; let them hear them.
But he said, No, father Abraham, but if someone goes to them from the dead, they will turn/realign.
He said to him, If they do not hear Moses and the Prophets, neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
